title: Complex Exponentials and Fourier Analysis
description: Why complex exponentials are the natural language for real signals, and the Fourier series/transform built from first principles rather than a table of pairs.
objectives: Explain why complex exponentials simplify the analysis of real oscillating signals, and the mirror-symmetry of a real signal's spectrum
Derive the Fourier series coefficient formula from the orthogonality of complex exponentials
Derive the Fourier transform as the continuous-frequency limit of the Fourier series, and state its duality and convolution properties
Recognize the standard time-domain/frequency-domain transform pairs (impulse, constant, rectangle/sinc, cosine)
